key_insight: eCNNTO speeds up density-based topology optimization by using an element-wise residual CNN to predict near-optimal densities from density histories, preserving spatial coherence while skipping most expensive FE iterations.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical surrogate for faster high-resolution structural design across varied loads, geometries, mesh sizes, and 2D/3D settings.
method: Element-based CNN with residual connections models neighboring-element spatial correlations.
Training uses final-stage density histories instead of early histories to improve efficiency and reduce data needs.
The network predicts element densities to bypass most topology optimization iterations.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports generalization across many test cases and iteration reductions of up to 90% in 2D and 97% in 3D.
watch_item: As with any surrogate-driven TO method, performance may depend on the representativeness of the small training set and careful validation is needed for unseen design regimes.
paper_title: eCNNTO: A Highly Generalizable ConvNet for Accelerating Topology Optimization
